channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes for 5th Straight Day
item.description: Though both sides appeared to be digging in, a top Iranian official seemed to leave the door open for a deal, saying, “We must fear neither war nor negotiations.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: A vessel at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Wednesday.

item.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
item.description: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva, Marc Santora and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Commander of Unmanned Aerial Systems Force, via Reuters
item.media:description: A screenshot taken from footage provided by Ukraine’s Unmanned Systems Forces showed a drone flying toward a ship at port in the city of Kerch in Crimea in early July.

item.title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
item.description: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Diosdado Cabello, Venezuela’s interior minister, welcomed a flight last year bringing Venezuelan migrants from Mexico.

item.title: For Argentina, a World Cup Semifinal Against England Isn’t Just Soccer
item.description: Wednesday’s contest for a spot in the final carries the weight of a 1986 contentious clash, a war over the Falklands and a national mythology built around Diego Maradona.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Lucía Cholakian Herrera

item.title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
item.description: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israelis gathering near a public shelter in Tel Aviv after a warning of incoming missiles from Iran in March.

item.title: France Votes to Allow Assisted Dying
item.description: Lawmakers approved a bill that will allow doctors to help terminally ill patients to die, adding France to a short list of nations that allow the practice.
item.dc:creator: Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:description: The National Assembly, the lower house of the French Parliament, as it voted on Wednesday to allow medically assisted dying.

item.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
item.description: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Laura Proctor/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The CN Tower in Toronto on Wednesday as smoke from wildfires enveloped the city.

item.title: Australia to Impose Energy and Water Guardrails on Data Centers Amid A.I. Boom
item.description: The country will also seek to protect the rights of creators of work used to train artificial intelligence models, as it aims to impose parameters on the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim and Laura Chung
item.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announced the new data center requirements at the University of Sydney on Wednesday.

item.title: As Argentina Tries to Win the World Cup, the Rest of Latin America Cheers Against It
item.description: Rather than rally behind the tournament’s last Latin American team, other countries in the region are confronting Argentina’s fraught claims to its own identity.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner
item.media:description: Colombia fans in yellow and Argentina fans in stripes watching Argentina playing Cape Verde earlier this month in Miami.

item.title: U.S. Presses China to Free American Seismologist Accused of Spying
item.description: President Trump has urged Xi Jinping to release the scientist, Youlin Chen, the man’s family says. Dr. Chen studied the detection of North Korean nuclear tests.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

item.title: Missing From Prince Harry’s UK Trip: A Reunion With Prince William
item.description: Harry is clearly repairing his relationship with King Charles III, but the rift with his brother remains unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prince Harry played wheelchair rugby on Friday in Birmingham, central England, to promote the one-year countdown to the 2027 Invictus Games, a sports event for wounded veterans.

item.title: Six Dead in an Elevator After Fire in Central Brussels
item.description: After a fire broke out at the Oxy building in central Brussels on Tuesday morning, six bodies were found in an elevator.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert
item.media:description: Emergency personnel outside a building in Brussels following a fatal fire on Tuesday.

item.title: Ann Widdecombe Was Killed in ‘Targeted Attack,’ UK Police Say
item.description: Counterterrorism police said that they were continuing to interview a 28-year-old man accused of killing Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the right-wing Reform U.K. party.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Ann Widdecombe speaking at a Reform party conference in Redruth, England, last year. She served as a Conservative minister in the 1990s.

item.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
item.description: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das, Saif Hasnat, Hari Kumar and Atul Loke
item.media:description: An Indian border guard patrols the border near Hakimpur in West Bengal last month.

item.title: Hong Kong Police Raid Independent Bookstores and Arrest 5 People
item.description: Other booksellers have been detained in recent months as part of a broad national security crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.media:description: Police officers loading confiscated items from Have A Nice Stay, an independent bookstore in Hong Kong on Wednesday.

item.title: The Race for A.I. Data Centers Arrives in India
item.description: India is developing new data centers to secure a foothold in the global A.I. race. Our South Asia bureau chief, Mujib Mashal, discusses how a new Google campus’s potential use of the region’s already strained energy and water resources is creating anxiety among local residents.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal, Sutton Raphael and June Kim

item.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
item.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Hari Kumar
item.media:credit: Priyadarshini Ravichandran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The port city of Visakhapatnam in the state of Andhra Pradesh is rapidly transforming into India’s coastal gateway for global tech infrastructure, fueled by massive A.I. data centers.

item.title: Europe Wants to Break Free From American and Chinese Technology. But How?
item.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Mark Landler
item.media:description: Sébastien Lecornu, the French prime minister, has called for France to increase its independence from American technology firms, announcing plans to replace Zoom with a French-developed version.

item.title: In Iran war, Trump Risks Another American ‘Forever War’
item.description: President Trump, who promised to “end wars,” not start them, may have fallen into a familiar presidential trap.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: A judiciary building damaged in the war as seen from the Golestan palace, in central Tehran, Iran, this month.

item.title: Iranian American Woman Held in Iran on Spying Charges Is Released
item.description: The dual Iranian-U.S. citizen, Dena Karari, had her passport seized in December 2024 and had not been able to leave Iran as she faced espionage charges.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: For England, the World Cup Final Remains Elusive
item.description: The nation’s years of pain continue.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: England’s loss prolongs a World Cup drought that has been a national pain point.

item.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Signals Door to Diplomacy Is Still Open
item.description: Facing intense divisions within Iran, Gen. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf both struck a defiant tone and argued that diplomacy could defend Iran’s interests.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, center, at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran this month.

item.title: Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, a Proponent of Drone Warfare, Steps Down
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov announced he was leaving the ministry on Wednesday after conflicts with generals and military contractors over the role of innovative weaponry.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov in Kyiv last March.

item.title: Guy Scott, Who Caused a Stir as White Leader of Zambia, Dies at 82
item.description: Two decades after the fall of apartheid in South Africa, he served briefly as Africa’s only white president.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell
item.media:description: Guy Scott in 2013, while serving as the vice president of Zambia. Earlier, as agriculture minister, he was credited with rescuing his country from potential famine after a severe drought.

item.title: Will Iran Be a Forever War?
item.description: President Trump may have fallen into the same trap that his predecessors did in Iraq and Afghanistan. Our chief diplomatic correspondent explains why.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes With No Sign of Backing Down
item.description: On the fifth straight day of fighting, the U.S. launched a 90-minute attack on Iran, while Tehran targeted Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu, Qasim Nauman and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A billboard depicting a burning White House and text reading “blood for blood” on the facade of a building in Tehran’s Palestine Square on Wednesday.

item.title: Billboard in Iran’s Capital Depicts Trump in a Coffin
item.description: Threatening imagery in Enghelab Square is nothing new, but has rarely taken such lurid form.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A billboard in Enghelab Square in central Tehran on Wednesday, with graffiti saying “We Will Kill Trump.”

item.title: U.S. Strikes Hit Greater Tunb Island in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military said on Wednesday it had attacked Iranian cruise missile storage and launch sites on Greater Tunb, one of several small islands in the Strait of Hormuz subject to a decades-old territorial dispute.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels at anchor in May off the coast of Oman, about 60 miles from Greater Tunb island in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Brazen Japanese Bears Are Breaking Into Homes and Raiding Pantries
item.description: Climate change and shifting demographics are driving the country’s Asiatic black bears closer to humans.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

item.title: The Vague Clause That Helps Explain the U.S.-Iran Fight Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran has done little to calm tension, in part because they disagree on what it actually means.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Wednesday.

item.title: Oil, Shipping, Flights: Disruptions Are Back as U.S.-Iran War Reignites
item.description: Higher prices for energy, food and air travel are likely to persist as the two sides escalate their clash over the Strait of Hormuz and launch airstrikes in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships at the Khor Fakkan Container Terminal, in the United Arab Emirates, on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Farnaz Fassihi and Alan Yuhas

item.title: Former Child Care Worker in Sydney Facing Over 300 Abuse Charges Is Identified
item.description: A Sydney court lifted the suppression order on identifying a man accused of abusing more than 100 children over 16 years.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: At War With Iran Again, Trump Finds an Opponent He Cannot Easily Dominate
item.description: The president is accustomed to forcing other countries to bend to his will. But he is struggling to come up with a strategy to extract Iranian concessions as the cease-fire he brokered collapses.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A banner in Tehran last week threatening President Trump.

item.title: Iran’s Cyberattackers Tracked Phones of U.S. Military Personnel in the Mideast, Data Suggests
item.description: Researchers say Iran’s cyberwarfare capabilities have grown more advanced and aggressive during the war with the United States and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iran used cellphone signals to track U.S. military members at the start of the war, according to newly released data.

item.title: Expelled from China
item.description: A New York Times correspondent was kicked out of the country after reporting on the struggles of everyday Chinese. Here’s her story.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tiananmen Square in Beijing in May.

item.title: Trump Drops Plan to Tax Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Fighting with Iran Escalates
item.description: The president’s proposal to impose a fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz threatened to increase global energy prices.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Jenny Gross and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump scrapped a plan on Tuesday to impose a 20 percent fee on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Death Leaves Fate of Russia Sanctions Bill Uncertain
item.description: Supporters in both parties hope the measure to penalize Russia, championed by Senator Lindsey Graham, will gain momentum in his honor.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham spoke to reporters in a visit to Kyiv, Ukraine, last week, in his last public act.

item.title: Iran’s Former Leader Denies Times Report
item.description: The Times reported that Israel had planned to reinstall Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader after the war.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, the former president of Iran, during a news conference in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Israel Strikes Police Post, Killing 7, Gaza Officials Say
item.description: Hamas says the strike hit Gaza’s civil security, while Israel says it targeted “terrorists.” Israel has stepped up attacks in Gaza recently as a cease-fire with Hamas has floundered.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:description: The bodies of some of those killed in an Israeli strike on a police station on Tuesday in Jabaliya, in northern Gaza.

item.title: Trump Paid $2 Million by South Korean Company Facing Trade Investigation
item.description: The payment illustrates the minefield Mr. Trump has created by maintaining personal financial ties with foreign businesses while he is in office.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton and John Yoon
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump maintains personal financial ties with nearly 30 different business ventures with counterparties worldwide.

item.title: Antonio Rattín, Whose World Cup Expulsion Led to Penalty-Card System, Dies at 89
item.description: The ejection of the Argentine soccer star during a 1966 match against England led to the introduction of yellow and red cards.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: India Condemns Deadly Iranian Strike on Commercial Ship
item.description: India issued a diplomatic protest after an Indian sailor was killed and dozens more were injured in an Iranian attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu and Mujib Mashal
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Indian-flagged tanker carrying crude oil that transited through the Strait of Hormuz, offloading at a terminal near Mumbai in April.

item.title: Inky Mark, Former Canada MP, Arrested After Police Find 439 Guns and Cannon in His Home
item.description: Inky Mark, a former conservative lawmaker in Manitoba, was charged with offenses including firearm trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: Inky Mark in Ottawa in 2005. He served as a member of Parliament from 1997 to 2010.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Meet to Advance Peace Talks, as Broader Truce Unravels
item.description: Expectations are low for the latest round of U.S.-mediated talks, aimed at ending months of war between Israel and t Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Rome, Italy, as U.S.-mediated talks between Israel and Lebanon resumed on Tuesday.

item.title: Jeffries Says He Will Oppose Proposal to Cut Off U.S. Aid to Israel
item.description: The House Democratic leader said he would not seek to persuade other Democrats to vote against the measure, which has divided the party amid a deep rift over support for Israel.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries at the Capitol on Monday.

item.title: Bloomberg Loses Landmark Libel Case in Singapore
item.description: A judge ruled in favor of two Singaporean ministers in a closely watched defamation trial seen as a significant test of the country’s libel laws and their implications for press freedom.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee
item.media:description: John Fraher, left, a senior executive editor for Bloomberg, and Low De Wei, a Bloomberg journalist, leave court in Singapore in April.

item.title: Europe, Flexing Muscle in Ukraine, Is Still on Sidelines in Iran
item.description: In a show of collective strength, President Emmanuel Macron of France hosted European allies at the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris. Yet Europe is still a hostage to America’s foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vehicles of the French Army’s Ninth Marine Infantry Brigade on the Champs-Élysées during the annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday.

item.title: China Purges Top Official Over Corruption and Sex Charges
item.description: Ma Xingrui is the third member of the Politburo to be purged since 2022. He once led the Xinjiang region and was previously head of China’s space program.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.category: Central Commission for Discipline Inspection (Communist Party of China)
item.media:description: Ma Xingrui at a meeting in Beijing last year. He had been the Communist Party leader of the far western region of Xinjiang before his downfall.

item.title: Trump’s Canceled Plan to Charge a Toll in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
item.description: A day after President Trump announced a hefty 20 percent fee on cargo through the waterway, he reversed his decision.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil and Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump on Tuesday retreated from his plan to levy a toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz as a way for the United States to recover the cost of providing military protection to vessels using the waterway.

item.title: In Bangkok Bar Blaze, Signs of Fatal Lapses Repeated Across the World
item.description: After a fire killed 30 people, the scrutiny has turned to whether the venue used flammable foam on its ceilings and had blocked exits.
item.dc:creator: Verena Hölzl, Phuriphat Dejsuphong, Kittiphum Sringammuang and Sui-Lee Wee
item.category: Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao (Bangkok, Thailand, Bar)
item.media:description: Flowers and offerings outside Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao on Tuesday. About 30 people died after a fire broke out at the bar on Sunday night.

item.title: Ukraine Wants to Make Its Own Antimissile System, Not Just Patriots
item.description: The Ukrainian firm Fire Point has produced weapons that hit Russian territory. Its next goal, developing an antimissile system, is a much bigger challenge, experts said.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Missiles manufactured by the Ukrainian defense firm Fire Point and video footage of a burning Russian refinery at the company’s exhibition stand at a defense industry conference in Kyiv last month.

item.title: A Heated Rivalry Over Bull Mascots Is Dividing a Brazilian Town
item.description: Deep in the Amazon rainforest, a heated rivalry has split a town for a century. It’s got nothing to do with sports or politics.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Dado Galdieri
item.media:description: The arena in Parintins, Brazil, showing both red and blue bulls on the facade before hosting a series of performances to determine the annual winning bull.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Max Bearak and Ishani Desai

item.title: Israel’s Regime-Change Plan for Iran
item.description: Here’s the wild back story behind a failed plan to install Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s next leader.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in 2024.

item.title: Hamas Forces Interfered With Aid Distribution, U.N. Says
item.description: A raid by armed personnel led the World Food Program to suspend its operations in one part of Gaza, the U.N. said. Hamas disputed the accusations.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israel’s war against Hamas has left homes largely destroyed in Khan Younis in southern Gaza.

item.title: Trump Reimposes Naval Blockade, as U.S. and Iran Escalate Strikes
item.description: After several days of attacks, Tehran and Washington have effectively reverted to the state of open conflict that existed before the United States and Iran agreed to a cease-fire in June.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Leo Sands and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships sailing near the Strait of Hormuz off the eastern coast of the United Arab Emirates at Khor Fakkan on Monday.

item.title: Hungary’s Parliament Votes to Remove Orban Loyalist From Presidential Post
item.description: Peter Magyar, Hungary’s prime minister, defended the extraordinary move, which has been criticized by human rights groups and by allies of Viktor Orban, the former right-wing leader.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: The Hungarian Parliament passed an amendment to the Constitution that would remove President Tamas Sulyok from office as president, a largely ceremonial role. People gathered to protest against the measure in Budapest on July 9.

item.title: U.K. to Change Law to Allow Deportation of ‘Grooming Gang’ Leader
item.description: The British government on Monday laid out steps enabling the deportation to Pakistan of Shabir Ahmed, the leader of a gang that raped dozens of girls in Northern England.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Shabana Mahmood, Britain’s home secretary, in London last month.
